changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_unique_user_service_account to k8s_rbac_unique_service_account_per_user for clarity
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the HIPAA control for unique user identification. The naming convention mostly follows the pattern, but a slight adjustment was made to improve clarity and specificity. The functions map well to Kubernetes RBAC capabilities, which can enforce unique identities and auditing.
naming_improvements: Specify 'per_user' in the function name to clearly indicate the requirement for unique service accounts for each user.
title: 164_312_a_2_i: 164.312(a)(2)(i) Unique user identification
step1_reasoning: The HIPAA control 164.312(a)(2)(i) requires unique user identification, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that each user or service has a unique identity. This can be enforced by using unique service accounts for each application or user, avoiding shared service accounts, and auditing user identities through RBAC policies. Kubernetes RBAC can be configured to ensure that service accounts are not shared and that user identities are properly logged and audited.
